name: Mobile-first UI redesign — Design System & Implementation Rules
scope: Define global design tokens (colors, radius, shadow, typography, spacing, animation)
Document layout architecture (mobile header, bottom tab, desktop sidebar, main content)
Create implementation rules for page building (component usage, styling, responsive, accessibility)
Provide anti-patterns and checklist for daily development

completed: Phase 10: V2.1 contract hardening and primitive API expansion

notes: Phase 7 (Profile Page) completed on 2026-05-11. Refactored Profile/Settings Page with mobile-first UX: removed redundant Shortcuts card (duplicated Memberships functionality 100%), consolidated onboarding CTA into Memberships empty state, reordered sections by usage frequency (Profile → Account → Memberships), added visible page title (text-xl md:text-2xl), applied card hover effects (border-transparent + hover:border-primary/20 + hover:shadow-md), elevated card titles to text-lg font-semibold, ensured all buttons and links meet min-h-11 (44px) touch targets, tightened container gap to gap-4 md:gap-6. Also fixed pre-existing calendar.tsx type error (removed invalid 'table' class name from react-day-picker v10 classNames) which unblocked build. Phase 8 (Auth Pages) completed on 2026-05-11. Verified liquid glass design integrity across sign-in, sign-up, public-layout, auth-panel, and auth-field. Identified that a subsequent cleanup commit (3acc15d) accidentally reverted the global Input component's liquid glass styles (border-white/20, bg-background/50, dark:border-white/10, dark:bg-white/5, focus glow). Restored liquid glass styling to all 5 auth inputs via explicit className overrides without refactoring the pages. Added subtle mobile refinement: sign-up footer now uses flex layout with flex-wrap (matching sign-in) for better text/link wrapping on narrow screens. Phase 9 (Landing Page) completed on 2026-05-11. Reviewed and refined mobile spacing, replaced hardcoded text-blue-950 with semantic tokens (bg-primary-foreground text-primary) for dark mode contrast compliance, reduced excessive mobile gaps in How It Works (gap-16→gap-10 md:gap-12) and Social Proof (gap-12→gap-8 md:gap-24), tightened card padding on mobile (p-8→p-6 md:p-8), and added subtle hover effects to testimonial cards for tactile consistency with other app pages. On 2026-05-12, follow-up contract hardening and primitive expansion aligned design-system docs to the V2.1 spec, expanded base primitives with additive variant/size/tone/surface APIs, and established a first consumer migration proof through dialog sizing. Later that same day, the shared forms/dialogs rollout phase migrated representative dialog shells, form structures, selection controls, and feedback states away from consumer-side visual restyling: shared dialogs now use DialogContent size='default', representative forms and filter controls now use primitive sizing and card/separator composition, onboarding form shell styling was reduced to layout-only composition, and representative Empty states now use surface='outline' while preserving layout-only min-height classes. Focused migration tests were added for dialog shells, form/selection consumers, and feedback-state contract usage to lock the rollout patterns.
